quiz.title: Error Spotting / Sentence Improvement - SSC Selection Post XIII 2025

questions.question: Identify the word in this sentence that contains an error. He jumped in the pool with his clothes on.
questions.explanation: Option A: clothes does not best fit the requirement of the question. Option B: pool does not best fit the requirement of the question. Option C: jumped does not best fit the requirement of the question. Option D: in fits the requirement of the question. **Option D** correctly applies: it is the grammatically correct choice for the sentence.

questions.question: Find the part of the sentence that contains an error: The application forms which were downloaded from the portal (1)/ and submitted after verification (2)/ is being processed in batches (3)/ under administrative supervision. (4)
options: The application forms which were downloaded from the portal (1)
questions.explanation: Option A: The application forms which were downloaded from the portal (1) does not best fit the requirement of the question. Option B: and submitted after verification (2) does not best fit the requirement of the question. Option C: is being processed in batches (3) fits the requirement of the question. Option D: under administrative supervision (4) does not best fit the requirement of the question. **Option C** correctly applies: it is the grammatically correct choice for the sentence.

questions.question: Choose the most suitable option to replace the underlined part of the sentence. If the sentence is already correct, select 'No substitution required'. One of the most valuable paintings **<u>were stolen</u>** from the gallery last night.
questions.explanation: Option A: was stolen fits the requirement of the question. Option B: have stolen does not best fit the requirement of the question. Option C: had stolen does not best fit the requirement of the question. Option D: No substitution required does not best fit the requirement of the question. **Option A** correctly applies: it is the grammatically correct choice for the sentence.

questions.question: Choose the sentence that correctly uses the comparative degree.
options: This is the most easiest task.
This task is more easier than the previous one.
This task is easier than the previous one.
This task is the more easier than the previous one.
questions.answer: This task is easier than the previous one.
questions.explanation: Option A: This is the most easiest task. does not best fit the requirement of the question. Option B: This task is more easier than the previous one. does not best fit the requirement of the question. Option C: This task is easier than the previous one. fits the requirement of the question. Option D: This task is the more easier than the previous one. does not best fit the requirement of the question. **Option C** correctly applies: it is the grammatically correct choice for the sentence.

questions.question: Identify the sentence that uses the positive degree of comparison.
options: She is the most intelligent student in the class.
She is more intelligent than her classmates.
She is as intelligent as her classmates.

questions.explanation: Option A: She is the most intelligent student in the class. does not best fit the requirement of the question. Option B: She is more intelligent than her classmates. does not best fit the requirement of the question. Option C: She is as intelligent as her classmates. fits the requirement of the question. Option D: She is the more intelligent student in the class. does not best fit the requirement of the question. **Option C** correctly applies: it is the grammatically correct choice for the sentence.

questions.question: Amit: Where were you, Akash? Akash: I didn't mean to leave you in the lurch. I thought we had canceled our meeting. What does Akash mean by saying, "I didn't mean to leave you in the lurch"?
options: Akash didn't want to leave Amit in a difficult situation.
Akash was busy with some urgent work.
Akash forgot about the meeting completely.
questions.answer: Akash didn't want to leave Amit in a difficult situation.
questions.explanation: Option A: Akash didn't want to leave Amit in a difficult situation. fits the requirement of the question. Option B: Akash was busy with some urgent work. does not best fit the requirement of the question. Option C: Akash forgot about the meeting completely. does not best fit the requirement of the question. Option D: Akash was intentionally avoiding Amit. does not best fit the requirement of the question. **Option A** correctly applies: it is the grammatically correct choice for the sentence.
